title: Pinning key posts and resources in a channel
what_it_is: Pinning fixes an important post or tab to the top of a Teams channel, so it stays visible and easy to find instead of getting buried under newer posts.
why_use_it: It means a learner can always find the most important resource for a channel — like a key document or a set of instructions — without needing to scroll back through the whole conversation history.
how_to: Open Teams and go to the channel containing the post you want to pin.
Find the post in the conversation, then select the three dots (⋯) that appear when you hover over it.
Select Pin from the menu that appears.
The post now stays fixed near the top of the channel, marked with a pin icon, so it's the first thing learners see.
To pin a file or resource as its own tab instead, select the + at the top of the channel, choose the file or app, then select Save — the tab stays visible across the top of the channel for everyone.
To remove a pinned post, select the three dots (⋯) on it again and choose Unpin.
Keep pinned content to genuinely key resources — pinning too many things makes it harder to spot the ones that matter most.
